title: Duration::from_mins is nightly-only unstable API in init_vdd_engine_if_enabled
description:
## Severity
Critical
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/main.rs:619-622
## Violation
The reqwest client builder uses an unstable nightly-only API.

```rust
let http_client = reqwest::Client::builder()
    .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_mins(2))
    .build()
    .unwrap_or_else(|_| reqwest::Client::new());
```

`std::time::Duration::from_mins` is gated behind the unstable `duration_constructors` feature (`#![feature(duration_constructors)]`) and is not available on stable Rust. This binary will fail to compile on stable toolchains, blocking CI and end-user installation. Additionally, the `unwrap_or_else(|_| reqwest::Client::new())` silently drops the timeout configuration if the builder fails — a configured 2-minute VDD timeout becomes the default (no timeout), which is exactly the bug crosslink #496 was supposed to prevent.

## Mandated refactor
Replace with stable equivalent and surface the build error:

```rust
let http_client = reqwest::Client::builder()
    .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(120))
    .build()?;  // propagate the failure rather than silently dropping timeout
```

If propagation is infeasible at this call site, return `None` and log a warn so callers know VDD is disabled rather than running with infinite timeout.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
